title: The Second Sunday of Easter
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## April 16, 2023 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is the Second Sunday of Easter in the church year, falling within the season of Easter (also called Eastertide), which runs from Easter Sunday through Pentecost. The Revised Common Lectionary for this date includes Acts 4:32-35 (the early church sharing all things in common), 1 John 1:1-2:2 (fellowship and the cleansing power of Christ's blood), and John 20:19-31 (the resurrection appearance to the disciples and Thomas's doubt). The Gospel reading centers on "Doubting Thomas," making it one of the most recognizable Sundays in the Christian calendar.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church in 2023 was navigating the aftermath of the 2022 General Convention's significant actions on sexuality and marriage, including the approval of rites for blessing same-sex unions and the removal of marriage being defined as between a man and a woman. The Diocese of Colorado, encompassing Denver and surrounding areas, was led by Bishop Kspecially Bolding-Lovett (elected in 2021), and the cathedral itself is the seat of the diocesan bishop. No major diocesan elections or crises were occurring in April 2023, though broader Anglican Communion divisions over LGBTQ+ inclusion remained an ongoing tension.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver in mid-April 2023 was experiencing typical spring weather with temperatures in the 50s-60s Fahrenheit, though the region had dealt with significant moisture and the potential for severe weather earlier in the spring season. The Colorado General Assembly was in its final weeks of the 2023 legislative session (concluding May 10), with various bills under debate. Downtown Denver and the Capitol Hill neighborhood where the cathedral is located were focused on typical spring activities, with no major local crises dominating the news cycle in the immediate week before April 16.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The week before April 16, 2023 saw the continued banking crisis fallout with ongoing concerns about regional bank failures following Silicon Valley Bank's collapse in March; President Biden signed legislation to raise the debt ceiling and suspend it until January 2025, a major political achievement after weeks of brinkmanship with House Republicans led by Speaker Kevin McCarthy. The Idaho governor signed a bill banning gender-affirming medical care for minors, adding Idaho to the growing list of states restricting such care. Former President Donald Trump faced ongoing legal challenges, with his federal indictment on classified documents charges expected within weeks (it was unsealed April 20, 2023). The 2024 presidential race was heating up, with Trump's primary lead expanding despite legal troubles.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The war in Ukraine was approaching the one-year mark of Russia's invasion (February 24, 2022), with major fighting ongoing around Bakhmut and concerns about renewed Russian offensives after winter; NATO remained focused on Ukraine support and strengthening its eastern flank. Tensions remained high between the U.S. and China, with ongoing concerns about Chinese spy balloons and broader great-power competition. North Korea conducted a suspected ballistic missile test in early April as tensions with South Korea escalated.

CULTURAL MOMENT

The United States in April 2023 was in a moment of heightened cultural and political polarization, with the debt ceiling conflict symbolizing deep partisan divides and the banking crisis creating economic anxiety. The 2024 election season was beginning in earnest despite being nearly two years away. Sports fans were following the beginning of the MLB season and the NBA playoffs (which would begin mid-April), with the Denver Nuggets defending their 2023 NBA championship. There was a general sense of institutional stress—political gridlock, economic uncertainty, unresolved questions about AI regulation following ChatGPT's viral adoption—alongside ordinary spring renewal, making for a somewhat anxious but hopeful cultural moment.
